summary: Final comprehensive validation of CollectPay after captcha+honeypot, Resend email integration, new header buttons (Empezar/Registrarte), Colombina client and 'Empresas que confían en nosotros' title. Backend: 24/24 pytest tests pass (auth 5, content 5, contact 7 incl. captcha valid/wrong/honeypot/too-fast/email-noop, security headers + rate limiting 6, root 1). Frontend: header buttons render with correct https://app.collectpay.co/#/auth/login + target=_blank, clients section shows ALL 8 companies (Hogaru, Brinks, Cluvi, Netactica, Domesa, TVS, AMS, Colombina) with new title, contact form math captcha works (wrong → error toast + auto-refresh, correct → success toast + form reset), ES↔EN toggle works, WhatsApp button present, admin login works and the just-submitted 'Test E2E User' lead appears at the top of the Leads tab (75 total). Mobile (390px) renders hamburger menu with Empezar+Registrarte. No critical issues.

minor.issue: Datetime tz bug from iteration 2 is FIXED (server.py:249-251 now coerces naive last_attempt to UTC-aware before subtraction). Verified by test_login_enforces_5_per_minute returning 429.

Captcha validation only triggers when BOTH captcha_answer AND captcha_expected are present (server.py:347). A client that omits captcha_expected would bypass captcha entirely. Today the frontend always sends both, but server-side captcha should be authoritative (generate captcha on backend, store in session/cookie, compare against server value) instead of trusting client-provided 'expected'.

form_started_at is fully client-controlled - any bot can set it to (now - 5000). Pair with a server-issued nonce/timestamp for real protection. Not blocking - honeypot + captcha together are good enough for now.

issues: WhatsApp tooltip bubble (¡Hola! ¿Necesitas ayuda?) overlaps the open mobile menu drawer near the bottom of the viewport on mobile, partially hiding the Registrarte button at first glance. Consider auto-hiding the tooltip when the mobile menu is open.

action_items: (Security hardening, optional) Move captcha generation to the backend so the expected answer is not client-controlled.
(Polish) Hide WhatsApp tooltip while the mobile hamburger menu is open so it doesn't overlap the Registrarte button.
critical_code_review_comments: Captcha is client-authoritative (frontend sends both captcha_answer and captcha_expected). A trivial bot can send matching values. Backend should issue a signed/server-side captcha token. Honeypot+timing still help.
RESEND_API_KEY empty path is correctly handled (silent no-op + WARN log, submission still persists with real ObjectId). Verified by test_submit_contact_email_failure_doesnt_break_submission.
CORS still uses allow_origins=['*'] together with allow_credentials=True (server.py:401-404). Browsers will reject this combo for credentialed requests; tighten to explicit FRONTEND_URL.
ContactSubmission doesn't validate phone format; sanitize_string is only applied AFTER pydantic dump. Not a security issue but consider regex validation for phone.
send_lead_notification catches exceptions but logs them at error level only - good. Resend uses asyncio.to_thread so it doesn't block. ✅
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (added 4 new contact tests: wrong captcha returns 400, honeypot filled → spam-filtered, too-fast submit → spam-filtered, email no-op doesn't break submission)
seed_data_creation: Created a 'Test E2E User' contact submission visible in admin Leads tab; multiple 'TEST_RL_BURST_*' and 'TEST_LEAD_*' leads also exist from automated tests. Recommend periodic cleanup of TEST_-prefixed submissions in db.contact_submissions.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 24 backend tests pass. Captcha+honeypot+timing anti-spam all verified end-to-end. Admin dashboard correctly shows new submissions. Header has both Empezar (text link) and Registrarte (gradient button) pointing to https://app.collectpay.co/#/auth/login with target=_blank. Clients grid shows 8 companies including Colombina under the new 'Empresas que confían en nosotros' title. Only follow-ups are SECURITY hardening (server-side captcha) and a minor mobile polish (hide WhatsApp tooltip when mobile menu is open).
rca of the issue: N/A - no bugs found in this iteration. Previous datetime/tz bug in brute-force protection is now fixed (verified).
